item.title: Anti-robot techniques can be nice but the problem is, they're not static 摘要
item.description:
The author proposes an anti-robot tactic called 'robots don't POST', which requires visitors to submit a simple HTML form via POST to receive a cookie for accessing web pages, as most crawlers only perform GET requests. However, this approach is a temporary measure in an ongoing arms race with malicious crawlers. The author notes that maintaining a low-attention website is increasingly difficult, as operators must actively participate in this arms race or risk their site being overwhelmed by bad crawlers, leading to high bandwidth costs or site destruction. The situation adds to the challenges of running personal websites, similar to the earlier issue of comment spam.
标签: anti-crawler, web security, robots, HTTP POST, arms race, website maintenance, low-attention web, crawler mitigation, web development, security tactics

item.title: The History of ‘OK’ 摘要
item.description:
The article traces the origin of 'OK' to a 19th-century linguistic fad for deliberate misspellings and abbreviations, where 'all correct' became 'oll korrect' and then 'o.k.' The term was popularized by Martin Van Buren's 1840 presidential campaign, where his nickname 'Old Kinderhook' led to 'OK Clubs.' The author prefers 'OK' over 'okay' or 'ok,' especially in UI labels, citing Apple's Human Interface Guidelines.
标签: etymology, linguistics, history, language, abbreviations, slang, presidential campaign, UI design, spelling, cultural trends

item.title: Trump Mobile Website Exposed the Number of Pre-Orders — Both Completed and Abandoned — and the Associated Customer Information 摘要
item.description:
Trump Mobile's website exposed customer data, including names, addresses, and phone numbers of people who filled out preorder forms. The vulnerability was discovered by an Australian individual and reviewed by Columbia professor Jonathan Soma, who noted the site used a common e-commerce model that recorded both completed and abandoned preorders. The exposed data showed 27,224 potential preorders, but Soma suggested the actual number of completed purchases was likely lower due to abandoned carts. Trump Mobile stated it was investigating with independent cybersecurity professionals.
标签: Trump Mobile, data breach, cybersecurity, e-commerce, privacy, customer data, vulnerability, preorder, investigation

item.title: Thieves Are Texting Threats to Victims of iPhone Theft in London 摘要
item.description:
A New York Times report details how iPhone theft in London escalates beyond the initial crime: thieves use stolen phones to threaten victims' families, demanding Apple ID removal to resell the devices. The article highlights that only iPhones are targeted, with Android phones often returned. It notes Apple's Stolen Device Protection, now default in iOS 26.4, but the story omits this feature. The piece underscores the organized nature of iPhone theft rings and the severe harassment victims face.
标签: iPhone theft, London crime, Apple ID, Stolen Device Protection, iOS 26.4, organized crime, phone theft, cyber threats, New York Times, Daring Fireball

item.title: Solving the board game Quoridor 摘要
item.description:
The author presents significant advances in solving the board game Quoridor, using novel techniques to fully solve almost all board configurations with area ≤ 28 (e.g., 5x5, 8x3, 7x4) for most wall counts on a consumer laptop. Key findings include: odd-height boards are not always second-player wins due to a trade-off between parity and tempo advantages; even-height boards are uniformly first-player wins; the 8x3 board with 3 walls per player is a forced draw from the starting position; and various geometries show unusual win patterns, such as 4x7 alternating between players and 7x3 never transitioning to a first-player win. A full results table for odd-height boards is provided.
标签: Quoridor, board games, game theory, combinatorial game theory, solving, AI, parity, tempo, draw, algorithm

item.title: Awarding Jay Haynes His Being Right Points for Predicting Apple Hitting $3 Trillion in Market Cap 摘要
item.description:
In 2014, Jay Haynes predicted Apple would reach a $3 trillion market cap within 10 years, a bold claim when Apple was worth $450 billion and no company had hit $1 trillion. Apple achieved this milestone in just 8 years, by 2022. John Gruber revisits Haynes's analysis, noting it holds up well, including its application of Clayton Christensen's disruption theory to iPhone and iPad. Gruber awards Haynes 'Being Right Points' for the accurate prediction.
标签: Apple, market cap, prediction, Jay Haynes, John Gruber, disruption theory, Clayton Christensen, iPhone, iPad, business

item.title: Welcoming the Bhutanese Government to Have I Been Pwned 摘要
item.description:
Have I Been Pwned (HIBP) has onboarded the Bhutan Computer Incident Response Team (BtCIRT) as its 45th government partner. BtCIRT will use HIBP's free government service to monitor Bhutanese government domains for leaked credentials and compromised databases. This enables Bhutan's national cybersecurity team to identify and respond to cyber risks affecting government services and citizens, leveraging breach data to reduce exposure and prevent credential-based attacks.
标签: cybersecurity, breach notification, government, Bhutan, HIBP, CIRT, threat intelligence, credential leaks, data breach, incident response
